poem:
The cat sat on the mat
And watched the mouse run past.
The rat was sleek and fat
And feared the sun at last.
critique: The moment in line 2—"watched the mouse run past"—holds attention. The line break after "past" creates a pause. Line 3 introduces "sleek" effectively. The "mat" / "fat" / "past" / "last" rhyme holds. The poem is complete. This poem has found its shape.

poem:
Roses are red
Violets are blue
Sugar is sweet
And so are you
critique: Line 1 and 2 rely on a familiar pairing. "Sugar is sweet" in line 3 shifts to abstraction. The closing "And so are you" does not land because the preceding lines do not establish a specific "you." Revise line 3 to add a concrete image before the turn. This poem has not found its shape.
